evals.prompt: Read the approved spec, handoff, stale notes, source, exports, callers, and tests. Produce a codebase design for sync preview. Do not implement or write a plan.
evals.expected_output: A human-reviewable architecture model that exposes shared plan ownership, preview and execution interaction, semantic interfaces, caller knowledge, and the chosen boundary without full function bodies.
assertions: Grounds the current model in actual modules and public exports
Makes shared filtering and ordering ownership explicit
Explains preview and execution control/data flow and client avoidance
States interface semantics and caller knowledge, not only signatures
Rejects stale command, plugin, config, and TypeScript proposals

evals.prompt: Read the approved spec, handoff, stale notes, package exports, source, callers, and tests. Produce a codebase design for billing preview. Infer compatibility from code. Do not implement or write a plan.
evals.expected_output: A human-reviewable architecture model that preserves independent published contracts, exposes shared charge-plan ownership and gateway interaction, and rejects the stale monolith and adapter design.
assertions: Infers public normalize and select contracts from package exports and tests
Keeps normalization and selection independent from billing orchestration
Explains preview and execution interaction through one charge-plan policy
States semantic interface and caller-knowledge changes
Rejects BillingEngine, Stripe adapters, test deletion, and TypeScript migration

evals.prompt: Revise the existing sync-preview design after this human correction: SyncPlanner must be an independent logical module because a future scheduled job is a third caller. The CLI and sync executor both consume plan(records). The planner knows nothing about stdout or the client. Public exports remain unchanged. Do not implement code or write a plan.
evals.expected_output: A revised architecture model that applies the corrected module and exact interface without reopening alternatives, inventing rationale, or specifying unconfirmed scheduled-job behavior.
assertions: Uses an independent SyncPlanner with the exact plan(records) interface
Makes CLI and executor consume the planner without planner I/O knowledge
Does not reopen the corrected choice with alternatives or retroactive rationale
Does not invent scheduled-job execution behavior
